sections.paragraph:
σχοίνισμα, ατος, τό (LXX) lit. ‘a piece of land measured out by means of a measuring-line (σχοῖνος, σχοινίον)’, in our lit. only in imagery allotment (Hesych.; Etym. Mag. p. 740, 46) σχ. κληρονομίας αὐτοῦ the allotment that he acquired 1 Cl 29:2 (Dt 32:9).—DELG s.v. σχοῖνος.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Hesych Hesych , V a.d.—List 5

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

σχ.̠sc. = scilicet (one may understand, supply)

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 982
